---
title: "Practice: Flow And Attrition"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

```{r}
#| include: false
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

# What We Practiced

The module showed how to:

- define cumulative stages
- count observations retained at each step
- calculate stage-to-stage loss or percent retained
- plot attrition through a workflow

# Task

Pick one dataset and create:

1. a stage-count table
2. one attrition or retention plot
3. a short interpretation of where observations are lost

Suggested datasets:

- `data/real/nhanes_2021_2023_analysis.csv`
- `data/real/titanic_passengers.csv`
- `data/simulated/neighborhood_health_survey_analysis.csv`

# Work

```{r}
# Load data, create a stage-count table, and build one attrition plot.
```

# Interpretation

Write briefly: which restriction removes the most observations and what the final analytic sample represents.
